Minhyan Lexicon   Advanced
The accompanying lexicon is translated from Nichopomm Ssil, "The Royal Word Collection", which has the virtue of being by far the largest surviving Minhyan dictionary (14,787 words) and the vice of containing many dubious etymologies.  King Idryf commissioned it explicitly to demonstrate that Minhyan was far more regular than Harody, and his lexicographers often overzealously derived words from infixed compounds.  For instance, yajed, "musket", is described as derived from a compound meaning "small moss" (yad, "moss" + -je-, "diminutive"), when scholars before and since have classified it as an independent root derived from Proto-Minhyan *i-ashet.  Unfortunately, not enough is known of Old Minhyan to determine the accuracy of other etymologies, such as acholf, "canary", from alf, "reed" + -cho-, "collection"; it is possible that acholf first meant "collection of reeds", then "bird of the reeds", then "canary", but this is not certain.  When reviewing etymologies, therefore, reader beware.

abel [< *abil.] n. Idea, invention.
abel [< abel, "idea, invention".] v. Invent, think up.
abell [< all, "service" & -be-, "action".] n. Attendance, waiting, service.
abell [< *abili-.] Hundred thousand.
abemurr [< arr, "aviate" & -be-, "action" & -mu-, "tool".] n. Aeroplane.
aberorr [< arr, "aviate" & -be-, "action" & -ro-, "professional".] n. Aviator.
aberr [< arr, "aviate" & -be-, "action".] n. Aviation.
abo [< *apo.] n. Brother.
abocu [< abo, "brother" & -cu-, "in-law".] n. Brother-in-law.
abocuna [< abo, "brother" & -cu-, "in-law" & -na-, "female".] n. Sister-in-law.
abona [< abo, "brother" & -na-, "female".] n. Sister.
abopi [< abo, "brother" & -pi-, "half".] n. Stepbrother, half-brother.
abopina [< abo, "brother" & -pi-, "half" & -na-, "female".] n. Stepsister, half-sister.
aborn [< arn, "post, mail" & -bo-, "thing".] n. Piece of mail.
abossa [< abo, "brother" & -ssa-, "vice".] n. Step-brother.
abossana [< abo, "brother" & -ssa-, "vice" & -na-, "female".] n. Step-sister.

uru [< *uru.] v. Be successful, prosper.
urucha [< uru, "stone" & -cha-, "cause/become".] adj. Petrified.
uruffu [< uru, "stone" & -ffu-, "augmentative".] n. Boulder.
uruho [< uru, "be successful, prosper" & -ho-, "small-item".] n. Prosperity, success.
urui [< *urue.] n. Acrobat.
urui [< urui, "acrobat".] adj. Acrobatic.
urui [< *urue.] n. Apostle.
urui [< urui, "apostle".] adj. Apostolic.
uruibe [< urui, "acrobat" & -be-, "action".] n. Acrobatics.
uruiffe [< urui, "apostle" & -ffe-, "quality".] n. Apostleship, apostolate.
uruje [< uru, "stone" & -je-, "diminutive".] n. Pebble.
urujecho [< uru, "stone" & -je-, "diminutive" & -cho-, "collection".] n. Stone-chippings, broken stones, chippings.
uruteho [< uru, "be successful, prosper" & -te-, "opposite" & -ho-, "small-item".] v. Decline, recede.
uruteho [< uru, "be successful, prosper" & -te-, "opposite" & -ho-, "small-item".] n. Decay.
urwen [< *urwin.] adj. Gallant.
uryi [< *uri-e.] v. Lecture.
uryiro [< uryi, "lecture" & -ro-, "professional".] n. Lecturer.
uryod [< *uri-od.] n. Fantasy.
ussar [< *usar.] adj. Small-minded, petty.
ussarr [< *usarh.] n. Crowbar.
usscar [< *uskar.] v. Camouflage.
usscar [< *uskar.] n. Cushion.

ussgal [< *usgal.] v. Kick, recalcitrate.
ussgar [< *usgar.] adj. Peremptory, resolute, firm, categoric.
ussgar [< *usgar.] n. Antimilitarism.
ussgarr [< *usgarh.] n. Pastor.
ussgeri [< *usgire.] adj. Covered with blood, blood-stained.

ussill [< ull, "ford" & -ssi-, "capable".] v. Survive, experience, live through.

ussos [< *usos.] Since, ever since.

usspalas [< *uspalas.] n. Waste of time.

usst [< *ust.] n. Distribution.

usst [< usst, "impulse, access, impetus".] v. Impel.

usstent [< *ustint.] v. Saturate.
ussto [< *usto.] n. Circumstance.
ussto [< *usto.] v. Seduce, entice.

usstoladd [< *ustolath.] n. Wine bottle.
usstrad [< *ustrat.] v. Promote.
usstui [< *ustue.] n. Daisy.

uteld [< uld, "gentle, mild" & -te-, "opposite".] adj. Bleak.

yad [< yad, "moss".] adj. Mossy.
yado [< *i-ado.] adj. Organic.

yadro [< *i-atro.] v. Sell off.

yair [< *i-aer.] adj. Dumb, speechless, mute.
